preamble: The shift from the neutral schwa [ə] to the open-mid back unrounded vowel [ʌ] is a primary feature of re-stressing in several English dialects. In varieties where [ə] and [ʌ] are phonetically near-identical, the distinction is purely one of stress, allowing the sounds to alternate depending on the sentence focus.

examples.note: Re-stressing of the weak form results in the caret quality.
Systematic restoration of the full vowel in stressed contexts.
